'''Darren Fleary''' (born 2 December 1972) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s, and [[Coach (sports)|coach]]. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]], [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]], as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Darren Fleary's birth was registered in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], [[England]].

After finishing his playing career, Fleary worked as a prison officer at [[Armley Jail]] in Leeds,<ref>{{cite web |title=Tackling new challenges |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/news/west-yorkshire-news/tackling-new-challenges-5041520 |website=Examiner Live |access-date=30 April 2023 |date=6 February 2008}}</ref> and also coached junior teams at [[Newsome Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Former Great Britain prop Darren Fleary at heart of Newsome Panthers' Under 13s recruitment |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/amateur/former-great-britain-prop-darren-4940577 |website=Examiner Live |access-date=30 April 2023 |date=6 October 2012}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Fleary started his professional career with [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]], signing from amateur club Moldgreen in September 1991.<ref>{{cite book |editor-last1=Fletcher |editor-first1=Raymond |editor-last2=Howes |editor-first2=David |title=Rothmans Rugby League Yearbook 1992-93 |date=1992 |publisher=Headline Publishing Group |location=London |isbn=978-0-7472-7906-8 |page=52}}</ref> He moved to [[Keighley Cougars]] in 1994.

===Leeds Rhinos===
In July 1997, Fleary was one of nine Keighley players who signed for [[Leeds Rhinos]] for a combined fee of £25,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds Rhinos nostalgia: Why Darren Fleary proved to be an unlikely hero |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-nostalgia-why-darren-fleary-proved-to-be-an-unlikely-hero-3474477 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=30 April 2023 |date=29 November 2021}}</ref>

Fleary played {{rlp|pr}} in [[Leeds Rhinos]]' 4–10 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] during [[Super League III]] at [[Old Trafford]], Manchester on Saturday 24 October 1998.

Fleary played in [[Leeds Rhinos]]' 52–16 victory over [[London Broncos]] in the [[1999 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League IV]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], London on Saturday 1 May 1999, and played left-{{rlp|pr}} in the 18–24 defeat by [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2000 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League V]] at [[Murrayfield Stadium]], Edinburgh on Saturday 29 April 2000.

===Later career===
Fleary joined his hometown club [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2003. He was chosen as the club's captain for the 2004 season, following the departure of [[Steve McNamara]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Fleary ready to seize both honour and responsibility |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/fleary-ready-seize-both-honour-5092381 |website=Examiner Live |access-date=30 April 2023 |date=28 January 2004}}</ref> He spent his final season with [[Leigh Centurions]] in 2005, where he was also appointed captain.<ref>{{cite web |title=New skipper misses opener |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/5795632.new-skipper-misses-opener/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=30 April 2023 |date=10 February 2005}}</ref>

===International honours===
Fleary played twice [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Leeds Rhinos in 1998 against New Zealand during the [[1998 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|1998 Kiwis tour]]. He also won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Leeds Rhinos in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] against Australia (sub), Russia, and New Zealand (sub).
